---
mode: agent
description: The CLI lens over the project's durable audit & change log — list the last N actions, search by session/date/keyword, copy or re-run a past prompt, toggle to the meaningful-change timeline, or open the interactive viewer.
---
You are running the **auditlog** workflow (`knowledge/audit-and-change-log.md`). The project keeps a durable, committed history in `docs/audit/` — append-only JSONL (`audit-log.jsonl`, `change-log.jsonl`) that every skill writes to, so a new session reads what was done and decided instead of starting blind. This skill is a **reader/dispatcher** over `docs/ai-forward-pack/scripts/audit-log.py`; it adds no behavior and does **not** log its own browsing.

Map the user's request to the matching subcommand: **see** recent activity → `list --n <n>` (`--kind change` for decisions); **search** → `search --keyword "<term>" | --session "<id>" | --since YYYY-MM-DD --until YYYY-MM-DD` (`--kind change` for decisions); **redo a past prompt** (the headline capability) → find the entry, then `get --id <al-NNNN> --field prompt` to recall the **exact prompt verbatim**, show it, and offer to re-run it now or hand it back to copy — never paraphrase a recalled prompt; **toggle to changes** → `list/search --kind change` (each decision's title, rationale, artifacts, git before/after); **open the viewer** → `render` then point to `docs/audit/index.html` (timeline · search · copy-prompt · full-history/changes toggle; works over file://); **suggest unlogged changes** → `suggest` (new ADRs/notes + decision-signalling commits not yet in the change log), offering to promote any with `change …`. Present lists/searches as a compact table (id · datetime · session · label · summary); confirm before re-running anything that changes the repo. If `docs/audit/` does not exist yet, say so — the history is created the first time any skill (or `audit-log.py append`) runs.

This skill **reads** — it writes no artifact, no frontmatter, no index, and appends nothing to the logs (it is exempt from the Discoverability and Audit mandates, like the pack-lifecycle skills). The history it reads is itself the graph node `docs/audit/audit-log.md`, maintained by the writing skills.

${input}
